resources.string: Are you sure? All progress will be lost.
Are you sure? Your progress will be lost.
Still there? Your session will end in 10 seconds.
Low battery detected. Your progress has been saved.
Test your maths skills from basic arithmetic to algebra and geometry.
Explore science from states of matter to chemical reactions and physics.
Practise grammar, vocabulary, comprehension and literary devices.
Revisit key historical events, figures, causes and effects.

string-array.name: math_foundation_questions
string-array.item: Round 4.67 to the nearest whole number.
What is the LCM of 4 and 6?

string-array.name: math_foundation_explanations
string-array.item: 3/4 = 75%. Multiply numerator by 100 divided by denominator.
4.67 rounds up to 5 since .67 is above .5.

string-array.name: math_advanced_questions
string-array.item: What is the area of a circle with radius 7? (use π ≈ 3.14)
What is the gradient of the line y = 3x - 2?
What is the interior angle sum of a hexagon?

string-array.name: math_advanced_explanations
string-array.item: Find two numbers that multiply to 6 and add to 5: 2 and 3. So (x+2)(x+3).
In y = mx + b, m is the gradient. Here m = 3.
Pythagorean theorem: a² + b² = c² for right-angled triangles.

string-array.name: science_foundation_questions
string-array.item: What are the three states of matter?
What force pulls objects towards Earth?
What is the unit of force?
Which gas do plants absorb during photosynthesis?
What is the boiling point of water in Celsius?
What is the freezing point of water?
What type of energy does a moving object have?
Which planet is closest to the Sun?
What is the process plants use to make food?

string-array.name: science_foundation_explanations
string-array.item: The three states of matter are solid, liquid, and gas.
Gravity is the force that pulls objects toward Earth.
Plants absorb CO₂ (carbon dioxide) during photosynthesis.
Water boils at 100°C at standard pressure.
The nucleus controls cell activities and contains DNA.
Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun.
Photosynthesis is the process plants use to make food using sunlight.

string-array.name: science_advanced_questions
string-array.item: What type of reaction is combustion?
What is Newton\'s second law of motion?
What is the role of decomposers in an ecosystem?
What is the chemical formula for water?
What happens to particles when a substance is heated?
What is the difference between speed and velocity?
What subatomic particle has a negative charge?
What is the law of conservation of energy?

string-array.name: science_advanced_a
string-array.item: Energy cannot be created or destroyed

string-array.name: science_advanced_b
string-array.item: Energy is always lost as heat

string-array.name: science_advanced_c
string-array.item: Energy can be created from matter

string-array.name: science_advanced_explanations
string-array.item: Combustion releases heat energy — it is an exothermic reaction.
Newton\'s 2nd law: Force = mass × acceleration (F = ma).
Decomposers break down dead organisms returning nutrients to the soil.
Water is H₂O — two hydrogen atoms bonded to one oxygen atom.
Ohm\'s Law: Voltage = Current × Resistance (V = IR).
Heating causes particles to gain energy, move faster and spread apart.
A food chain shows the transfer of energy through feeding relationships.
Velocity includes direction; speed does not.
Electrons are negatively charged subatomic particles.
The law of conservation of energy states energy cannot be created or destroyed.

string-array.name: english_foundation_a
string-array.item: A word that describes a noun

string-array.name: english_foundation_b
string-array.item: A word that describes a verb

string-array.name: english_foundation_explanations
string-array.item: A proper sentence starts with a capital and ends with a full stop.
A noun is a person, place, or thing.
The correct spelling is "receive" — i before e except after c.
An adjective is a word that describes a noun.
Questions end with a question mark (?).
Run is a verb — it describes an action.
Joyful is a synonym of happy — it means the same thing.
Hot is the antonym (opposite) of cold.

string-array.name: english_advanced_questions
string-array.item: Identify the literary device: "The wind whispered through the trees."
What is the main purpose of a thesis statement?
What is the effect of short sentences in writing?
Which is an example of a simile?
What is the purpose of a topic sentence in a paragraph?

string-array.name: english_advanced_a
string-array.item: A direct comparison without using "like" or "as"
To state the main argument of an essay
When audience knows more than characters
A narrator who knows all characters\' thoughts
Repetition of consonant sounds at word starts
Introduces the main idea of the paragraph

string-array.name: english_advanced_b
string-array.item: When a character says the opposite of what they mean
A direct quote from the text
A narrator who is a character in the story

string-array.name: english_advanced_c
string-array.item: A figure of speech using exaggeration
When events turn out opposite to expectations
Repetition of a word for emphasis
The night was a dark blanket

string-array.name: english_advanced_d
string-array.item: A word that sounds like what it describes
When a character lies to another

string-array.name: english_advanced_explanations
string-array.item: A metaphor directly compares two things without using "like" or "as".
Personification gives human qualities to non-human things — wind cannot truly whisper.
A thesis statement presents the main argument of an essay.
Dramatic irony is when the audience knows something the characters do not.
Short sentences create urgency, tension, and impact in writing.
An inference is a logical conclusion drawn from evidence in the text.
An omniscient narrator knows the thoughts and feelings of all characters.
Alliteration is the repetition of the same consonant sound at the start of nearby words.
"He ran like a cheetah" uses "like" — that makes it a simile.
A topic sentence introduces what the whole paragraph will be about.

string-array.name: history_foundation_questions
string-array.item: In which year did World War II end?
Who was the first person to walk on the Moon?
In which year did World War I begin?
Who was Martin Luther King Jr.?
Which country was Adolf Hitler leader of?
When did the French Revolution begin?

string-array.name: history_foundation_a
string-array.item: A wall dividing East and West Berlin
A civil rights leader in the USA
A large territory under one ruler
A famous ship that sank in 1912
A political tension between the USA and USSR

string-array.name: history_foundation_b
string-array.item: A wall around all of Germany
A trade war between two countries

string-array.name: history_foundation_c
string-array.item: A wall between France and Germany

string-array.name: history_foundation_explanations
string-array.item: World War II ended in 1945 with Germany\'s surrender in May and Japan\'s in September.
Neil Armstrong became the first human to walk on the Moon on July 20, 1969.
The Berlin Wall was built in 1961 to divide communist East Berlin from West Berlin.
World War I began in 1914 following the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand.
Martin Luther King Jr. was a leading figure in the American civil rights movement.
An empire is a large territory or group of nations ruled by a single power.
The Titanic was a British ocean liner that sank on its maiden voyage in April 1912.
Adolf Hitler was the dictator of Nazi Germany from 1933 to 1945.
The Cold War was a period of geopolitical tension between the USA and the Soviet Union.
The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille.

string-array.name: history_advanced_questions
string-array.item: What was the main cause of World War I?
What was the significance of the Magna Carta (1215)?
What was the impact of the Industrial Revolution?
Which event triggered the start of World War II?
What was the significance of the Treaty of Versailles?
Who led the Russian Revolution of 1917?

string-array.name: history_advanced_a
string-array.item: It limited the power of the king
First Black president of South Africa
Genocide of 6 million Jews by Nazis

string-array.name: history_advanced_b
string-array.item: It gave all citizens the right to vote
First Black president of the USA

string-array.name: history_advanced_c
string-array.item: A trade dispute between France and Germany
It established the right to free speech
Leader of the ANC in exile
A form of slavery in the USA
A religious conflict in the Middle East

string-array.name: history_advanced_d
string-array.item: A border dispute between Russia and Austria

string-array.name: history_advanced_explanations
string-array.item: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand in Sarajevo triggered a chain of alliances leading to WWI.
Magna Carta was a landmark document limiting royal power and establishing rule of law.
Nelson Mandela was an anti-apartheid activist who became South Africa\'s first Black president in 1994.
The 1929 Wall Street Crash led to bank failures, unemployment, and the Great Depression.
The Industrial Revolution transformed societies from agricultural to industrial economies.
Germany invaded Poland on 1 September 1939, prompting Britain and France to declare war.
Apartheid was a system of institutionalised racial segregation in South Africa (1948–1994).
The Treaty of Versailles (1919) formally ended WWI and imposed heavy penalties on Germany.
Vladimir Lenin led the Bolshevik Revolution that overthrew the Russian Tsar in 1917.
The Holocaust was the systematic genocide of approximately 6 million Jewish people by Nazi Germany.
